_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn how feelings affect our behaviour and how thinking about consequences can help when people disagree.

_source.w: We learn how feelings affect our behaviour and how thinking about consequences can help when people disagree.
y: Understanding our emotions helps us control our behaviour in difficult moments.
Thinking about what might happen next helps us make better decisions.
These skills help us solve disagreements peacefully.
eg: We ask 'What might happen if I do this?' before making a choice.
We explore how feeling upset can make us say things we do not mean.
We practise thinking about the consequences of our actions before we act.
We discuss how taking a deep breath can help us think more clearly.
We read a story where thinking about consequences helped someone make the right choice.
We use a 'what would happen if...' chart to explore different choices and outcomes.
